Empires & Puzzles Bartholomew Review: The Spicy Bell Pepper of Doom

Bartholomew isn't just a salad ingredient; he's a meta-breaking tank buster. With a passive that spawns fiends every turn and a skill that eats buffs for breakfast, discover why this Bell Pepper is making waves in Empires & Puzzles.
Bartholomew Review: Is This Bell Pepper Too Hot to Handle?
Bartholomew: The Bell Pepper That Bites Back
Meet Bartholomew, the hero who finally answers the age-old question: "Can a bell pepper kill a dragon?" Spoiler alert: Yes, and it will probably counterattack while doing so. Hailing from the
hero is the face of the

family (even if botanists insist he's a fruit). Released in late 2025, he brings a unique "Harvest" mechanic and a passive skill so aggressive it feels like he's trying to take over your garden—and your defense team.

Let's be honest: it’s a giant, angry bell pepper with a shield. The design is a bold departure from the usual brooding warriors and ethereal elves. He looks like something out of a fever dream in the produce aisle—round, red, and surprisingly menacing. The "Bell Pepper Barbarian" aesthetic is equal parts ridiculous and terrifying, proving that Small Giant Games is willing to embrace the absurdity of vegetable warfare. It’s certainly... crisp.
Bartholomew boasts a modern stat line with

. While slightly eclipsed by the absolute latest December 2025 releases (which push 1320+ Attack), he remains firmly in the top tier. His bulk is significant, allowing him to survive long enough for his menacing passives to take root. He is not a glass cannon; he is a sturdy, fibrous tank-buster.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Vegetable family is all about growth, but Bartholomew’s personal kit is where the real power lies.
The bonus buffs the Attack/HP of fiends summoned by the family. While nice, it requires multiple Vegetables to be effective. It’s a cherry (tomato) on top, but not the main course.

Bartholomew represents the "Passive Creep" era. His raw special skill stats are strong but balanced; however, his
ability to generate infinite value (fiends) every turn without spending mana puts him ahead of older heroes who rely solely on tile matches.

He is a hard counter to the "Growth" meta (Goblins) and "Undispellable" buffers. His passive pressure makes him relevant in almost every game mode. He isn't just a flavor of the month; he's a staple ingredient for modern offense.

Free fiends every turn force enemies to play defensively.
The "Harvest" mechanic is one of the few reliable counters to Growth and heavy buff stacks.
Punishes silencers and mana blockers with self-healing and mana gain.
Self-healing via Harvest and resistance makes him deceptively tanky.
Without fiends on the board, his 300% hit is just "okay" for a modern Average speed hero.
The passive targets random enemies; it might target a hero who is about to die or one who has just been healed.
If the enemy has heavy anti-fiend tech (e.g., Grimsteel, newer lunar heroes), his passive value drops.
While strong, he is already being chased by the 1300+ Attack monsters of Dec 2025.
His Average speed becomes Very Fast, meaning he can keep Counterattack up 100% of the time and harvest buffs constantly.

Excellent for cracking stall teams or Goblin defenses.
His fiends deal damage and absorb healing (if any exists), but since there's no healing, they just act as permanent damage sources.
Overwhelm the enemy with minions and fiends. Bartholomew benefits from the chaos, and his passive damage softens them up for the others.
Goblins rely on Growth. Bartholomew eats Growth first. This team systematically strips the enemy of their extra stats while pounding them with damage.
Don't pair him with too many other "Counterattackers" if they overwrite his buff (though his is to "nearby," so positioning matters).

If you pulled Bartholomew, congratulations! You have a hero who can carry you through map stages simply by surviving. Level him immediately. Focus on keeping him alive with healers; let his passive fiends do the work for you.

Bartholomew is a tactical nuke against buff-heavy metas. He is worth the rings and potentially an Alpha Aether if you struggle against Goblins or pure-passive defenses. His ability to remove "Undispellable" buffs via Harvest is a mechanic that will age well.
The release of the Vegetable family caused quite a stir in the community.
Players noted the absurdly low 0.2% drop rate, with one user comparing pulling him to a "Zod Drop" from Diablo II—so rare it's unbelievable.
"They’re a pepper….. aren’t peppers fruits?" ignited a fierce botanical debate. One user lamented, "This is just so stupid. Literally everything; the art... the convoluted 'harvest' mechanic."
Despite the art criticism, the consensus on his kit was clear: "The passives are so good this is still going to be a meta hero."

Bartholomew is a hero who looks like a joke but hits like a truck. He forces opponents to rethink their buffing strategies and demands constant attention to his passive fiends. While his art might make you crave a salad, his gameplay will make your opponents salty. He is a top-tier utility attacker who will remain relevant as long as buffs and Growth exist in the game.
A spicy addition to any roster. Viable, dangerous, and nutritious.
A comprehensive review of Bartholomew, the 5-star Fire hero from the Vegetable family. We analyze his OP Seedling Summoner passive, the new Harvest mechanic, and his role in the current meta.
